observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended the following:
The Municipal Treasurer shall send a written notice to the aforementioned payees for them to be aware of their stale checks. Henceforth, practice sending written notice to the payees one month before the check becomes stale as required in Section 104 of GAM for LGUs.
If checks are returned by the payee, the Municipal Accountant shall request a certified copy of the DV from the Auditor for presentation to the Municipal Mayor who shall countersign the check. The cancelled check shall be reported and attached to the RCI prepared at the period of cancellation. The replacement check shall also be reported chronologically in the RCI.
If the payees are untraceable or did not respond to the notice, the Municipal Accountant shall make the necessary adjusting entries as follows:
For stale checks over 6 months but not over 2 years –
Cash in Bank-LCCA                              P xxxxx
Liability Account                                                    P xxxxx
For stale checks over 2 years (in compliance with Section 97 of Presidential Decree No. 1445)–
Cash in Bank-LCCA				         P xxxxx
Prior Years Adjustment*					        P xxxxx
(*to represent surplus adjustment)

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Sangguniang Bayan a list of cash in bank accounts that remained dormant for so many years with the request to close and transfer them to the General Fund.
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to close and transfer the said balances from the Trust Fund bank accounts to the General Fund bank accounts.
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to consider the balances as a source of fund in the preparation of the budget.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Re-activate the Inventory and Appraisal Committee to identify and appraise all properties whose costs cannot be reliably determined due to the unavailability of financial records. Require the committee to prepare an Appraisal Report and submit it to the Commission on Audit for Technical Review and Inspection.
Direct the Inventory Committee to dutifully conduct the annual physical count of all the property, plant, and equipment owned by the Municipality and to avail of the one-time cleansing of PPE per COA Circular No. 2020-06.
Direct the GSO and the Municipal Accountant to maintain the prescribed Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards, respectively, and to reconcile these with the RPCPPE to establish the correct amount of PPE in the financial statements.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Prepare manually the Statement of Financial Position to include the current portion of Loan Payable-Domestic; and,
Request the accounting software developer to enhance the software in accordance with the existing Accounting Standards.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the full disclosure requirement under IPSAS 30 for the financial liabilities in the Notes to Financial Statements.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Officials concerned:
Direct the Implementing Offices to hasten the implementation of projects under the 20% MDF so that optimum utilization thereof could be attained.
Create a Project Monitoring Team that would monitor the implementation of 20% Municipal Development Fund.
Direct the BAC to plan and execute the timely procurement of all identified priority development projects to guarantee the prompt accomplishment of the same. See to it that projects that are unlikely to be implemented in the current year are excluded from the list of priority projects.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Direct the Municipal Engineering Office to install signboards with complete project details on all Infrastructure Projects implemented by Administration and to require the winning Contractor on projects that underwent bidding to do the same pursuant to the existing regulations.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and other Officials concerned to undertake effective, efficient, and aggressive tax collection strategies to improve collection. Prepare and serve NDs for all delinquent taxpayers and avail of the remedies to enforce collection.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Convene the municipal officials concerned and other key stakeholders to plan the strategic location, design, budget, public market code or ordinance and other important matters to be discussed regarding the construction of a Municipal Public Market.
Determine the allocation (or outsourcing if warranted) of funds/budget for the construction of its building and design.
Enjoin the Sangguniang Bayan to support the project.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
The Implementing Offices concerned to prepare and submit a monitoring and evaluation report of the subject projects.
The Municipal Engineer to require the contractors concerned to submit explanation on the incurrence of delays in completing the projects and subsequently determine the appropriate action to be taken whether to issue a Warning Letter or to directly employ punitive actions prescribed by existing guidelines.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the HRMO must ensure that the functions and duties of the CoS/Job Order personnel must be well-defined and clear in the contract/job order, known and accepted by them prior to rendition of services to facilitate proper evaluation of work performed by these personnel; and, accomplishment reports should be evaluated vis-a-vis the terms/ services agreed upon in the contract.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMO to coordinate with the MDC in formulating the Municipality’s DRRM Implementing Plan.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Facilitate the establishment of the GAD M&E system as required in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 to help the officials concerned in the proper monitoring of the implementation of GAD policies and PPAs and the evaluation of the effectiveness of the same;
Direct the Officials concerned to ensure proper mainstreaming of GAD concerns in the local development plans, especially in the CDP.
Direct the GAD Focal Point Person to furnish the Audit Team with reports required in JMC 2013-01 to aid in the audit of GAD funds and PPAs.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
The General Services Officer to prepare and/ or amend the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) of the Municipality such that all procurement/purchases should be in accordance with an Approved Procurement Plan (APP).
The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to strictly comply with all the requirements stipulated in the New Procurement Law or R.A 9184 and its implementing guidelines and ensure the completeness of the supporting documents to be attached to the DV prior to submission to the Audit Team.
The Municipal Accountant to see to it that all payments for the purchase of goods shall be supported with proper and complete supporting documents.  Particular attention be given to the chronological dates the transactions occur to avoid suspensions in audit, thus fostering transparency of government transactions without creating any malice.
The Municipal General Officer to exercise due diligence in inspecting and accepting the goods to be received and ensure that these are complete in her custody.

Recommendations:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
Re-activate the Inventory and Appraisal Committee to identify and appraise all properties whose cost cannot be reliably determined due to the unavailability of financial records. Require the committee to prepare an Appraisal Report and submit it to the Commission on Audit for Technical Review and Inspection.
Direct the Inventory Committee to dutifully conduct the annual physical count of all the property, plant, and equipment owned by the Municipality and to avail of the one-time cleansing of PPE per COA Circular No. 2020-06.
Direct the GSO and the Municipal Accountant to maintain the prescribed Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards, respectively, and to reconcile these with the RPCPPE to establish the correct amount of PPE in the financial statements.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to compute for the proper depreciation of the PPE items through the preparation of a lapsing schedule and to subsequently prepare the corresponding adjusting entry.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant trace the transactions affecting the accounts to ascertain the correct composition of the accounts and take up the necessary adjusting journal entries in the appropriate books so that the accounts will be properly recorded and reported in the financial reports

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
Stop the grant of cash advances for purposes not included in the above-cited Circular.
Stop the grant of cash advances for purchases covered under negotiated/ emergency procurement.  These may be subject to suspension/disallowance in the succeeding post-audit of transactions.
Each end-user may appoint an employee as a canvasser to fast track the canvassing of purchase requests submitted to the General Services Office and to lessen the heavy workload of the GSO due to lack of manpower.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require the department heads concerned to submit the duly accomplished AAPSI containing the observations and the corresponding recommendation/s, as discussed in detail in Part II of CYs 2018 to 2020 AARs, and every AAR thereafter.

Recommendations:
We recommended that management observe and immediately consider the lacking minimum requirements on disaster preparedness and mitigation for the Municipality to be compliant with the minimum standard requirements, thus boosting its capacity to manage disasters.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to religiously abide by the rules set forth herein and submit the required quarterly utilization reports promptly.

Recommendations:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
Cause the immediate liquidation of outstanding cash advances by the accountable officers concerned, especially those advances that are long overdue.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to withhold the salary of accountable officers or impose a refund if liquidation is not possible anymore.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to cause the prompt submission of Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) for all bank accounts of the Municipality to the  Audit Team;
Request for a snapshot of accounts if bank statements are not yet available to hasten the conduct of monthly bank reconciliation.

Recommendations:
We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to direct:
The offices concerned with planning, budgeting, and accounting use the 20% DF in accordance with the rules, regulations, and guidelines.  Avoid transfer of funds in the form of subsidies.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to:
Submit the list of PPAs to the Audit team at the beginning of the year and the updated status of PPAs every end of the quarter;
Post relevant information in conspicuous places and in the venue where the PPA is located, and
Take cognizance and religiously abide by the rules set forth pertaining to PPAs in COA Circular No. 2013-4.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the transaction documents, financial statements, supporting schedules and other related reports are duly submitted to the COA Audit Team within the prescribed legal due dates.

Recommendations:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Local School Board to conduct adequate planning so that programmed projects/activities under SEF be reasonably attained during the year and to obtain optimum utilization of the available funds.
Ensure that the unexpended balance of Capital Outlay from the continuing appropriations is reprogrammed/re-appropriated in the ensuing year and the planned PPAs be incorporated in the revised AIP.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the DRRM Officer to conduct adequate planning so that programmed projects/activities under LDRRM Fund be reasonably attained during the current year and to obtain optimum utilization of the available funds.
Ensure that the unexpended balance of MOOE from the 70% mitigation fund of the current year is reprogrammed/re-appropriated in the ensuing years and the planned PPAs be incorporated in the LDRRMFIP and revised AIP. Also, include the planned Evacuation Center in the next budget to fully utilize the LDRRM Fund.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. To address the deficiencies in the identification, profiling and encoding of
beneficiaries
 Require the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO)
to investigate the inconsistencies in the records particularly the distribution
of assistance to individuals who were not in the encoded SAC Forms;
 Direct the MSWDO to create a task force to validate the existence and
qualification of the beneficiaries; and,
 Require the MSWDO to devise a process flow of transaction within their
office that would include review procedures.
b. To address the deficiencies in the documentary requirements
 Require the MSWDO and Municipal Accountant to submit the valid
identification of individuals who were granted with assistance but has no
valid identification attached to the payroll; and,
 Direct the MSWDO to create a task force to validate the qualification of the
beneficiaries who have attached Barangay Certifications only as proof of
identification.
c. To address the issue of the conduct of activities prior to the execution of MOA
 Clarify with the DSWD the reason for the delay of the execution of the
MOA and whether the Municipality has acted without authority during the
period of distribution.
d. To address the issue on the non-compliance of the provisions in the MOA
 Require the MSWDO and Municipal Accountant to submit a justification
as to the incurrence of delays in the fulfillment of the obligations in the
MOA.
e. To address the deficiencies in the internal controls
 Require the Internal Audit Office to conduct an evaluation of the internal
controls in the Municipality and to devise procedures, if there are none or
improve the same if there are breakdowns noted.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Instruct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO)
and Municipal Accountant to take cognizance with the guidelines issued by
the regulatory agencies concerned which affect the operations of the MSWD
Office through periodic viewing of the portal of these agencies or through
other activities that may be deemed appropriate;
b. Require the MSWDO and the Municipal Accountant to submit the OSCA
Identification of the individuals concerned to the Office of the Auditor;
c. Inquire from DSWD on the reasons for the delay of the MOA execution and
the authority of the LGU to distribute funds without MOA; and,
d. Require the MSWDO to evaluate the line of communication with other
departments to ensure the efficient exchange of information within the
Municipality.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. To address the budgetary constraints:
 Require the Municipal Health Office, Municipal Engineering Office and
Municipal Budget Office to identify the funding required after duly
considering the necessary activities for the operation of COVID-19
facility;
 Request the Sangguniang Bayan to provide budget augmentations, if
necessary, to fund the activities on the operation of COVID-19
Facilities; and,
 If there are funding gaps, these shall be submitted to Provincial
Government of Davao Occidental for resolution in coordination with
DOH, DILG and the DBM as provided in DOH-DILG JAO No. 2020-
001.
b. To comply with the Infrastructure Requirements:
 Instruct the Municipal Health Office to provide for the necessary
specifications required in the COVID-19 facility infrastructures;
 Require the Municipal Engineering Office to provide for: (a) a
construction plan duly considering the specifications submitted by the
Municipal Health Office; (b) total estimated cost of the facility; (c) the
submission of PPMP to the Bids and Awards Committee intended for
the procurement of materials; and,
 Request the BAC to facilitate the expeditious procurement of the
needed materials for the construction.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. To comply with the Supplies and Other Resources Requirements:
 Instruct the Municipal Health Office to: (a) identify the supplies and other
resources needed for the operation of the COVID-19 facilities; and, (b)
submit the PPMP to the Bids and Awards Committee intended for the
procurement of supplies; and,
 Request the BAC to facilitate the expeditious procurement of the supplies
and other resources needed for the operation of the COVID-19 facilities.
b. To comply with the Service Requirements:
 Coordinate with the Department of Health, through the Municipal Health
Office, in providing for mental health and psychosocial support services
(MHPSS) in COVID-19 facilities as required under DOH-DILG JAO No.
2020-001.
c. To comply with the Staffing Requirements:
 Require the Municipal Health Office to: (a) identify the staffing
requirements for the COVID-19 facilities; (b) provide for the estimated
funding requirements; and, (c) conduct pre-deployment orientations and
basic trainings to the health care providers;
 Instruct the Municipal Human Resource Office with the coordination of
the Municipal Health Office to facilitate the expeditious recruitment of
staff for the COVID-19 facilities; and,
 Request the Department of Health, with the coordination of the Municipal
Health Office, for the deployment of deputized doctors and nurses
required for the operation of the COVID-19 facility.
d. To comply with the Reportorial Requirements:
 Instruct the COVID-19 facility manager, through the Municipal Health
Office to prepare and submit the draft daily brief reports and submit the
same to the offices concerned;
 Require the Municipal Health Office to ensure that registration in the
Temporary Treatment and Monitoring Facility (TTMF) Tracker is
undertaken; and,
 Ensure that the DDC TTMF Application is updated.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Health Officer
to (a) require the submission of daily monitoring forms and status of individuals
subjected to quarantine and (b) secure the corresponding recommendation from
the Municipal Epidemiology and Surveillance Unit prior to the issuance of the
Certificate of Quarantine Completion.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following activities to improve the
contact tracing activities of the Municipality:
a. Local Government Unit and Municipal Epidemiology and Surveillance Unit
 Require the Municipal Health Office (MHO) and Municipal Epidemiology
and Surveillance Unit (MESU) to submit a detailed case investigation plan
and submit the same to the Office of the Municipal Mayor for approval and
dissemination;
 Direct the MESU to prepare a list of documents to be submitted to MESU
and subsequently archive the same for later reference and retrieval; and,
 Require the MHO to identify in the case investigation plan the personnel in-
charge of each activity the case investigation plan and disseminate the same
to the personnel in-charge.
b. Local Contact Tracing Team Leader and Co-Team Leader
 Direct the Local Contact Tracing Team Leader and Co-Team Leader to
devise a contact tracing plan to be followed in the subsequent contact
tracing activities and to submit the same to the Office of the Municipal
Mayor for approval and dissemination;
 Instruct the LCCT Leader and Co-Team Leader to conduct periodic
trainings or orientation for the members of the Local Contact Tracing Team
to keep them abreast with the latest developments in healthcare and other
protocols in handling COVID-19; and,
 Remind the LCCT Leader and Co-Team Leader to utilize the Demographic
Vulnerability Tool of the DOH in planning and implementation of tasks.
c. Members of the Local Contact Tracing team
 Require the members of the Local Contact Tracing Team to provide health
education to those close contacts of individuals diagnosed with COVID-19
and document the same for reference and reporting.
 Direct the members of the Local Contact Tracing Team to conduct daily
monitoring of close contacts for 14 days and submit the accomplished daily
forms to the Local Contact Tracing Team Leader and Co-Team Leader for
consolidation and submission to the MESU.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Health Officer
to coordinate with the MESU, Local Contact Tracing Team and BHERTs to observe
the timely submission of daily reports and to establish clear delegation of tasks and
responsibilities in accordance with existing guidelines.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Direct the members of the Local COVID-19 Task Force to prepare the Local
COVID-19 Task Force Daily Report and submit the same to the regulatory
agencies;
b. Require the Municipal Health Office and the Contact Tracing Teams to provide
timely and accurate information to the Local COVID-19 Task Force for the
preparation of the report; and,
c. Instruct the Municipal Health Office, the members of the Local COVID-19
Task Force and the Contact Tracing teams to keep abreast with the issuances
and requirements of regulatory agencies by attending conferences, browsing
agency websites or undertaking other means that may be deemed appropriate.

We recommended the following to the Municipal Mayor:
a. Request the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO)
to draft policies on: (a) recognition of the threats posed by COVID-19 related
health care wastes; and, (b) establishment of separate storage and collection
bins for COVID-19 related health care wastes;
b. Direct the MENRO to conduct monitoring on each barangay to ensure that
COVID-19 related health care wastes are segregated at the household level;
c. Request the Barangays under the Municipality to conduct an information drive
to raise awareness of households on proper hygiene practices, and safe
handling of infectious and contaminated health care wastes for collection;
d. Provide documentation on the segregation and storage activities for reference
and archiving.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Direct the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO)
to specify in the guidelines on waste management the important details on the
collection and transportation of COVID-19 related health care wastes
including, but not limited to the following: (a) manner of disinfection of
collection and storage bins; (b) schedule of collection; (c) prohibition of
manual segregation of their wastes; and, (d) mandatory wearing of personal
protective equipment for sanitation workers;
b. Instruct the Municipal Health Officer (MHO) and the Municipal Environment
Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) to conduct an orientation to waste
collectors on the proper handling of COVID-19 related health care wastes; and,
c. Provide documentation on collection and transportation activities for reference
and archiving.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Direct the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer and
Municipal Health Officer to submit the microbial laboratory tests results of
the treatment facility on COVID-19 related health care wastes to the DENR
and/ or DOH for approval.
b. Require the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer to
conduct an orientation to waste disposal personnel on the classification of
wastes being delivered for precautionary measures, maintaining a safe
distance from, and avoid direct contact with the wastes.
c. Provide documentation on treatment and disposal activities for reference and
archiving.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Require the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO)
and the Municipal Health Officer (MHO) to: (1) prepare a specific
Occupational Health and Safety Plan duly considering the risks faced by each
department in the Municipality and to submit the same to the Office of the
Municipal Mayor for approval and dissemination; (2) conduct: (a) orientation
on the proper usage and maintenance of PPEs; and (b) trainings on
Occupational Health and Safety with emphasis on the hazards in the
workplace, environmental and administrative controls and the properties and
characteristics of PPEs;
b. Direct the MENRO to provide disinfectants and hand sanitizers for every
waste collection vehicle; and,
c. Provide documentation of activities on Occupational Health and Safety Plan
implementation for reference and archiving.

We recommended the following undertakings to the Municipal Mayor to efficiently
and effectively manage the COVID-19 related health care wastes:
a. Establish a Composite Team in the Local Solid Waste Management Board
(LSWMB) that will take charge in the development of policies pertaining to
COVID-19 related health care wastes and for its implementation;
b. Require the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO)
and LSWMB to prepare a COVID-19 waste management plan and integrate
the same to the 10- year Solid Waste Management Plan of the Municipality.
c. Direct the MENRO to conduct orientation and other information drive about
the proper management of COVID-19 related wastes in barangays.
d. Require the MENRO and the Composite Team to devise activities to support
the Barangay Ecological Solid Waste Management Committees in the proper
handling of COVID-19 related health care wastes.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Require the Municipal General Service Officer (MGSO), Municipal Disaster
Risk and Reduction Officer (MDRRMO) and Municipal Accountant to inspect
the quality of rice grains and determine whether these are still good for
consumption;
b. Instruct the MGSO and MDRRMO to find a temporary facility that will be used
in the storage of rice grains; and,
c. Require the MDRRMO, the MGSO and the Municipal Engineer to submit a
proposal on the construction of a storage facility for approval and subsequently
submit the same to the Sangguniang Bayan for proper action.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Social Welfare and Development
Officer, Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer
(MDRRMO) and the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to submit
and reconcile their records in order to resolve the variance noted;
b. Require the Municipal Human Resource and Management Officer to review the
competencies of the MGSO and MDRRMO and to provide the needed trainings
or undertake other means necessary; and,
c. Instruct the MGSO and MDRRMO to establish internal control mechanism
within their departments or, if they would be found to be unfit to perform such
task, require other personnel competent to undertake such task to take charge.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Require the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO)
and the Municipal Disaster and Risk Reduction Management Officer
(MDRMMO) to prepare a database of Muslim families and communities in the
Municipality through the assistance of the Barangays and to develop programs
that will address their needs during the national health emergency; and,
b. Direct the MSWDO to keep abreast with the guidelines of regulatory agencies
by attending conferences, visiting agency website and other activities that may
be deemed appropriate.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Require the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO)
and the Municipal Disaster and Risk Reduction Management Officer to prepare
a list of persons with disabilities in the Municipality through the assistance of
the Barangays and to develop programs that will address their needs during the
national health emergency.
b. Direct the MSWDO to keep abreast with the guidelines of regulatory agencies
by attending conferences, visiting agency website and other activities that may
be deemed appropriate.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Direct the members of the Local COVID-19 Task Force to create a centralized
information and recording committee which will take charge in the pooling of
information, reports and documentation relating to the activities undertaken to
address the pandemic;
b. Require the members of the Local COVID-19 Task Force to provide the
documents pertaining to the implementation of the mandatory wearing of face
masks and other PPEs; and,
c. Request the Sangguniang Bayan to submit copies of ordinances passed in 2020
mandating the wearing of face mask and other personal protective equipment.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Request the Assistant Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to
provide for the identification documents of the recipients and any other proof
of residency and affiliation with TODA; and,
b. Assign to the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office any activities
that involves poverty alleviation or assistance in order to facilitate the
establishment of parameters in the identification and selection of beneficiaries.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Request the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO) to
submit the program design of the Special Education Assistance;
b. Require the Municipal Assistant Treasurer to gather the proof of identification
and proof of enrollment of the beneficiaries;
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit to our Office copies of the proof
of identification and proof of enrollment of the beneficiaries gathered by the
Municipal Assistant Treasurer;
d. Require the beneficiaries who received assistance: (a) beyond the threshold
amount; and, (b) multiple times, to refund the excess amount received; and,
e. Create a team to investigate the irregularities found in the distribution of
Special Education Assistance and provide sanctions to those individuals found
guilty of defrauding the Municipality by disqualifying the erring students to
receive future assistance and reprimanding the erring personnel concerned.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Require the Municipal Agriculturist to submit the documents related to the
ALPAS COVID-19 to the Office of the Auditor; and,
b. Establish policies in relation to the submission of documents to the Office of
the Auditor and provide the provision of withholding or suspension of salaries
in cases where the personnel concerned failed to submit the documents and
reports needed in compliance with Section 122 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the Sangguniang Bayan to
identify the benefits that may be given to employees, in coordination with the
Municipal Budget Officer and to provide the benefits due to them subject to the
financial capability of the local government unit.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) through its Secretariat to post
the emergency procurement activities under the Bayanihan Act in the GPPB
Online Portal; and,
b. Direct the BAC to be abreast with the issuances of the regulatory agencies
pertaining to procurement activities.

2. Direct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary demands to persons responsible to settle their respective suspension since this at year end remained unsettled within ninety (90) days after receipt by the accountable officer concerned, hence, this notice has matured into Disallowance pursuant to Section 82 of PD No. 1445 requiring the persons liable immediate payment thereof.
3. Enjoin compliance on the above COA Auditing rules and regulations to preclude this Office from issuing Notice of Disallowance on Notice of Suspension not settled on the prescribed period.

23. We recommended the immediate remittance of RPT shares to the Provincial Government of Davao Occidental including the Municipality’s Barangays shares.
24. Coordination between the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant must be established for it is required under Section 271 of R.A. 7160, thereby Institute a system that could improve the process of remitting RPT shares to concerned LGUs in a systematic manner.
observations.reason: It is required under Section 271 of R.A. 7160, thereby Institute a system that could improve the process of remitting RPT shares to concerned LGUs in a systematic manner.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend to the GSO or the Municipal Treasurer as the case may be to maintain stock cards for each item of materials and inventory supplies to account for the receipts and issuances of the same.
31. Consolidate weekly the Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) for which supplies and materials were issued using the Summary of Supplies and Material Issued (SSMI) and forward the same to the accounting office to be the basis of the Accountant in preparing Journal Entry Voucher and recording withdrawals in the Supply Ledger Cards.
32. We recommend to the Municipal Accountant to maintain Ledger Cards for various supplies and Materials Inventory and reconcile the same to the balance of the Stock Cards Maintained by the GSO or Municipal Treasurer as the case may be.

39. Direct the Municipal accountant to observe the following items of the COA Circular, to wit:
1.1 Item 4.0 – Coverage
1.2 Item 6.0 – General Guidelines
1.3 Item 7.0 – Specific Guidelines
1.4 Item 8.0 – Procedures in the write-off of dormant accounts; and
40. Enjoin compliance of the above-mentioned COA Circular.

observations.reason: Reiterated CY- 2018 AAR

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Require the officials concerned to review the contractual arrangements
entered between the Municipality and the individuals concerned; and,
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to evaluate the ability of the borrowers
to fulfill their obligations and to recognize the corresponding impairment
loss, if warranted.

We recommended to Management the following:
a. Issue a correspondence to the Bureau of Soil and Water Management
(BSWM) that the fund transferred to the Municipality was subject to a
Sandiganbayan decision;
b. Request a conference with the officers of the BSWM to discuss: (a)
existence of any other obligations by the Municipality from the fund
transferred based on the Memorandum of Agreement entered by both
parties, (b) the proper accounting of the fund, and (c) other matters that
Management may find necessary;
c. Provide appropriate disclosures in the Notes to the Financial Statements
pertaining to the liability indicating, among others, that the amount was
subject to a Sandiganbayan decision.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the direct the Municipal
Accountant to: (a) reconcile the accounting records with supplies and inventory
records of the General Services Office; and, (b) undertake the necessary adjusting
entries to reflect the result of reconciliation with the GSO.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to compute for the proper depreciation
of the PPE items through the preparation of lapsing schedule and to
subsequently prepare the corresponding adjusting entry, and;
b. Require the Municipal Accounting Office to maintain Property Ledger
Cards.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
a. Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and
the General Services Officer with the complete description and cost
segregation of road components for road projects; and,
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize the cost of each component
of the local roads with identified cost and subsequently provide the
proper depreciation thereof in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to review the monthly computation and remittance of the Retirement
and Life Insurance Premiums (50103010) account to ensure compliance with existing
regulations.

For  guidance  on  complete  documentation  for  evaluation   and auditoria review of contracts/POs, and the required technical inspection  of purchased  equipment,  we  attach  Checklist  for  Technical  Evaluation  of Equipment Procurement Contract  (Annex  3-I)  and  Checklist  for  Technical Evaluation of Delivered Equipment (Annex 3-H), both annexes to COA  Circular 2009-1 for easy reference on submission of transaction documents.

We further recommend full disclosure in the financial statements as at year-end on the restriction of cash in bank account  where  IRA  of  the  LGU  is credited directly, and certain percentage being made as collateral per  Loan Agreement executed by and between the LCE representing the LGU and the  LBP, Malaita Branch.
observations.managementAction: The complete loan transaction documents  including  paid  DV's/JEV's  were officially submitted to COA Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
The Municipal Accountant  should  closely  monitor  the  cash  advances granted to officers and employees by issuing  Demand  Letters  specially  to those with balances pertaining to prior years and  those  with  P50,000  and more, copy furnished this Audit Team for  coordinated  effort  to  implement the provisions of PD 1445 and COA Circulars on cash advances.

The management should stop granting cash advance  to  official  or  employee unless the previous cash advance given to him/her is  first  settled,  or  a proper accounting thereof is made, in  compliance  with  the  above-referred provisions of law.  A  certification  by  the  LCE,  duly  endorsed  by  the Municipal Accountant as  to  existence/non-existence  of  unliquidated  cash advance can serve as an attachment to support  subsequent  claims.

observations.recommendation:
An appropriate study shall  be  made  by  the  Human  Resource Office on the  system  of  hiring  Job  Order  (J.O.)/Contractual  employees taking into consideration the duties and responsibilities  assigned  to  the  existing number of regular employees and their capacity to perform  them  as  against the expected assignment and output of the job order employees  hired  in  order  to  prevent  duplication  of  work  and  for   equal/reasonable distribution of workloads. We require the following courses of action:
     a)  Require  the  Department  Heads  to  prepare  a  well-defined  job description and the specific duties and responsibilities for  every JOs as a way of determining the following:


1) Job Order requirements of the office/work assignment
 2) Whether work is aligned with the office function, plans and programs;
 3) Evaluation of periodic accomplishments;
 4) Job expertise needed in hiring employees

b) Require every job order employee, or  by  the  immediate  supervisor,  to prepare a monthly accomplishment report that shall be  in  accordance  with his job description/work assignment. It  shall  be  specific  in  terms  of quantity and description of duties and responsibilities.  This shall be made as basis in determining performance of the employee and in  any  promotion, for any available casual or regular position.

Also require the re-evaluation of work assignment of  Regular  employees  to determine if all other needed works/jobs can be performed  by  the  existing personnel and if they are still capable of doing their assigned task.
observations.managementAction: The  requirements  of  the  office/work  assignment complied.

observations.recommendation:
Purchases of supplies should  be  accounted using the perpetual   inventory
     a) method as prescribed in Section 114, Volume I, NGAS for LGUs;


     b) The Municipal Accountant shall  maintain  the  perpetual  inventory records  comprising  Supplies   Ledger   Cards   (SLC)   for   each commodity/stock;  while  the  Property  Custodian  shall   likewise maintain stock cards for supplies and shall consolidate the RIS for which supplies and materials were issued and prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and forward to Accounting unit for the preparation of JEV and record withdrawals in  the  Supplies Ledger Cards (SLC). This may, however, may be implemented  in  case  of the absence of space/bodega, that is thru Municipal  regulations on “shared-function responsibility” –  by  every  Office/Department supplies management system.

 (c.) Physical inventory shall be  conducted  on  semestral  basis,  and  be reported in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI).
 This shall be submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than January  31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively under Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGUs.

observations.recommendation:
The Municipality should establish GAD Focal Point  System  (GFPS) in accordance with the composition prescribed in item 4.0  (A)  (2)  of the  JMC # 2013-01 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA.

     a) Direct the Local Planning and Development Office to  spearhead  the setting up and maintenance of the GAD database to  serve  as  basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation. The GAD database can  either  be  manually  operated  or  developed through software and shall form  part  of  the  overall  Management Information System (MIS) of the agency.

     b) Formulate the GAD planning and budgeting  in  accordance  with  the steps and processes prescribed in item 4.0 (C) by the same JMC.

     c) Instruct the LGU  GFPS  to  assist  the  Local  Sanggunian  in  the formulation of a GAD Code.
